# Chronicle v2 Doc Pivot — Spec

**Status:** Draft (planning document)
**Date:** 2026-06-17
**Author:** generated by the AI agent during a 3-round interview with the user
**Goal:** execute a single docs-only pivot from the v1 simulation-first documentation to the v2 choice-driven CYOA authoring documentation, then commit and push the change.

---

## 0. Summary

The user pasted a new 25-section `ARCHITECTURE.md` describing **Chronicle v2: A Branching Fantasy Adventure Engine**. The new vision is fundamentally different from the current v1 documentation, which describes a deterministic persistent-reality simulation with autonomous NPCs, an LLM narration layer, and a 7-engine tick loop.

This spec captures the pivot plan. It does not itself rewrite any documentation file. The actual rewrite of the 5 in-scope doc files, followed by a single atomic commit and push to `origin/main`, is the execution phase that follows once this spec is approved.

**Commit shape:** one commit, all docs in scope, branch `main`, remote `origin`.

---

## 1. Why This Is a Doc-Only Pivot

The user's confirmed intent is:

> Full pivot — **docs only first**, code later.

The codebase currently implements the v1 simulation-first design (7 engines, 150 autonomous NPCs, LLM-first narration, SQLite persistence, deterministic tick loop). Migrating the code to a choice-driven CYOA authoring engine is a substantial multi-phase effort that involves a new module tree (`internal/engine`, `internal/story`, `internal/state`, `internal/events`, `internal/endings`, `internal/content`, `internal/ui`), retired simulation packages, and a new JSON-serialization save format. None of that code work is in this commit.

This commit is the **dead reckoning point** between v1 code and v2 docs. After this commit lands, the docs describe the v2 design; the code still implements the v1 design. Future phases migrate the code to match.

---

## 2. Authoritative Inputs

The pivot is anchored entirely to the user-pasted content (the message that contained the new doc text). Specifically:

| Input | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|
| New `ARCHITECTURE.md` (verbatim, 25 sections) | The user message's `[Pasted Text]` block, lines `# ARCHITECTURE.md … 25. Definition of Done` | Authoritative. Becomes the new `ARCHITECTURE.md` after light reconfirmation edits. |
| 3 rounds of `ask_user` answers | Captured in §3 below | Authoritative. All 11 sub-decisions pinned. |
| Current `README.md`, `PHASES.md`, `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md`, `docs/DETERMINISM.md` | Repo HEAD (commit `Fix describeWalkingNowhere interpolating raw user input into prose` lineage) | Read-only inputs. Their content is what gets replaced. |

The pasted doc has its own internal numbering (Vision, Design Goals, Core Loop, World State, Story Structure, Choice System, Conditions, Effects, Relationship System, Relationship Events, Reputation System, Inventory, Event System, Story Acts, Character Selection, Story Flags, Variables, Save System, Ending System, Planned Endings, Content Pipeline, Engine Architecture, Runtime Flow, Testing Strategy, Definition of Done). That structure is the **target shape** for the new `ARCHITECTURE.md`. We do not renumber it.

---

## 3. Pinned Decisions

These are the 11 decisions confirmed by the user across 3 clarifying rounds. **Every execution step below must conform to these.**

| # | Decision | Choice | Round | Source of authority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| D1 | Pivot scope | **Full pivot — docs only first, code later** | R1 | R1 answer |
| D2 | Content flavor (Dragon/Elara/Void Dragon) | **Keep as-is — named example content is canonical** | R1 | R1 answer |
| D3 | Module layout | **Adopt the new tree as the v2 target** (cmd/ + internal/{engine,story,state,events,endings,content,ui}/) | R1 | R1 answer |
| D4 | Sim-code fate in the doc | **Explicitly retire sim engines in the doc** (Population, Relationship, Marriage, Memory, Goal, Economy, Event — explicitly deprecated) | R2 | R2 answer |
| D5 | LLM policy in the doc | **Strict "no AI" — purge from v2 doc** (no OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY, no llm package, no doctor command in the v2 doc) | R2 | R2 answer |
| D6 | Input model | **Pure choice menu — no free text input** (`look`/`walk`/`talk`/etc. are not in v2; player navigates authored choices) | R2 | R2 answer |
| D7 | Persistence model | **Adopt the new minimal persistence** (JSON, one file, no migrations until v2.1) | R2 | R2 answer |
| D8 | Doc set in scope | **Replace every stakeholder doc** (ARCHITECTURE.md + README.md + PHASES.md + SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md + docs/DETERMINISM.md) | R3 | R3 answer |
| D9 | Commit shape | **One commit, all docs** | R3 | R3 answer |
| D10 | Numeric specifics (4 protagonists, 5 acts, 180-node sum, 12 endings, 3 romance targets, 4 factions, Kingdom/Mages/Dragons/Underworld) | **Aspirational, can be revised** (treat as working figures — content authoring is the next phase) | R3 | R3 answer |
| D11 | Determinism documentation | **Embed a small determinism section inside the new ARCHITECTURE.md**; delete or radically shrink `docs/DETERMINISM.md` | R3 | R3 answer |

If any of these flips during execution, the user must re-confirm — the spec is a binding plan.

---

## 4. Doc-by-Doc Replacement Plan

This is the only section that directly drives the actual rewrite.

### 4.1 `ARCHITECTURE.md` — **full rewrite, content drawn from the user-pasted text**

- **Action:** write_file replacement, content = the user's pasted 25-section text **with reconciliation edits per §6**.
- **Source:** the pasted text in the user's message.
- **Length target:** ~25 numbered sections (matching the pasted structure). The user-pasted text is approximately the existing ARCHITECTURE.md length.
- **Doc preamble:** replace the current "Chronicle — Architecture & V1 Specification" header with the user-pasted "Chronicle v2 — A Branching Fantasy Adventure Engine, Version: 2.0".
- **No companion-file linking** in the new doc preamble — the new doc does not link to `docs/DETERMINISM.md` (which is being gutted per D11) nor to `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` (which is being deleted per §4.4). Internal determinism lives inside this same doc (§6 below).

### 4.2 `README.md` — **full rewrite, top-to-bottom**

- **Action:** write_file replacement, content framed entirely around the v2 vision.
- **The current README's big issues:**
  1. Top banner: "immersive text adventure powered by a persistent reality simulation engine" → must become something like "a choice-driven fantasy adventure game with handcrafted branching stories".
  2. "What's in the box": currently describes 150 autonomous NPCs, 4 factions (Merchant Guild/Town Council/Faith of the Dawn/Frontier League), 5 axes (trust/respect/fear/attraction/loyalty) → must become a description of the v2 story (acts, endings, romance targets, companions Elara/Selene/Orion).
  3. "20+ verbs" REPL section: contradicts D6 (no free-text input). Must be removed.
  4. "The Player Experience" / "Immersive narration — LLM-first" / "NPC dialogue" sections: must reflect authored-content (no LLM).
  5. "The Engine" / "7 simulation engines" / "Determinism" section: must reflect v2 engine schema.
  6. "Project Layout" tree: currently lists `internal/{action, conversation, core, intent, lineage, llm, narrator, persistence, repl, simulation, tick, worldpack}`. Per D3, must be replaced with the v2 tree: `internal/{engine, story, state, events, endings, content, ui}`.
  7. "CLI Examples" / `chronicle doctor` / `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`: must be removed (no LLM in v2 per D5).
  8. "API Keys" section: must be removed.
- **What to preserve:** the "Build, Test, Run" block (`make build`, `make test`, `make vet`) stays — those are real commands. "Build, Test, Run" remains valid after the rewrite.
- **Hyperlinks to other docs:** currently README → `PHASES.md`. With PHASES.md being rewritten (§4.3), the link target shifts. The link itself stays valid (the file path doesn't change), so the link line is preserved.

### 4.3 `PHASES.md` — **full rewrite**

- **Action:** write_file replacement, content framed around v2 phase plan.
- **What to retire from the current PHASES.md:**
  - All Phase 26 sub-phases (save/load round-trip, trim events, 10-seed stress, CourtAction perf, engine iteration order) → these are v1 sim-engine perf/validation phases; in v2 the relevant phase list is different.
  - All Phase 27–33 phase entries — current Phase 33 is "immersive text adventure transformation", which is now redundant (the new doc IS the CYOA transformation in doc form).
  - All Phase 34–40 entries (`--no-llm`, World AI weekly, LLM cache, XDG world directory, branch/switch/timeline CLI, export/import, SQLite test fix, list/delete/pack commands, final v1 acceptance) → these were v1 sim polish. `World AI`, `--no-llm`, LLM cache, XDG directory, SQLite test deadlock, `chronicle list`, `chronicle delete` are all v1 sim-engine concerns; in v2 none of them apply or are reframed.
  - The "Suggested next 3 phases" list at the bottom (Phase 38, Phase 30, Phase 31) → must be replaced with a v2 phase list (see §6.2 of this spec).
  - The "Status after Phase 33" walkthrough block at the bottom (CLI examples + immersive REPL commands) → must be removed (REPL with `look`/`walk`/`talk` contradicts D6).
- **What to introduce in the new PHASES.md:**
  - Phase 34 series: the code-migration phases that bring the v1 sim code in line with the v2 docs. (Naming and content of these phases is intentionally NOT pre-specified in this spec — see §6.2.)
  - At least one v1-engine-retirement phase (Population, Relationship, Marriage, Memory, Goal, Economy, Event removal — per D4).
  - A "Post-Pivot Backlog" section that lists follow-up engineering work items.

### 4.4 `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` — **delete in this commit**

- **Action:** `git rm SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md`. The file's content describes the v1 simulation tick lifecycle (advance time, aging engine, economy, population, relationship, goal, event, memory, persistence flush, narration decision — in that order). In v2 there are no engines. There is no sim clock. There is no tick. The Runtime Flow in the new ARCHITECTURE.md §23 replaces this entire document.
- **No replacement file** is created. The ARCHITECTURE.md §23 (Runtime Flow) is the canonical place for "what the runtime does", and it covers StoryNode → Evaluate Conditions → Render Story → Render Choices → Player Selection → Apply Consequences → Trigger Events → Advance Story.

### 4.5 `docs/DETERMINISM.md` — **shrink to a stub or delete**

- **Action (preferred):** write_file replacement with a one-paragraph stub that says "Chronicle v2 determinism contract is documented in `ARCHITECTURE.md` §<the embedded determinism section>; this file is preserved as a redirect-only stub for old links."
- **Action (acceptable alternative):** `git rm docs/DETERMINISM.md`.
- The bulk of the existing file's content (RNG streams seeded by `(world_seed, tick, entityID)`, engine order `Population → Relationship → Marriage → Memory → Goal → Economy → Event`, the 7-engine hash methodology, the float normalization, the map-iteration order audits, the `core.WorldHash` SHA256 fingerprinting, the replay test guarantees) is **all** about the v1 simulation. There is nothing in v2 that maps one-to-one onto that content. The relevant v2 determinism guarantee is **"save → load round-trip preserves the WorldState `SaveGame` JSON object literally"**, which is one paragraph, not 460 lines.

---

## 5. Reconciliation Rules Where Pasted Doc vs Repo Reality Differ

These are the spots where the pasted text references things that are not actually present in the codebase, or where the pasted text contradicts stale v1 doc residue. Each is a single explicit edit required during the rewrite.

### 5.1 The pasted doc's `cmd/` block in §22

```text
cmd/
internal/
    engine/
        runner.go
    story/
        nodes.go
        choices.go
        conditions.go
        effects.go
    state/
        world.go
        save.go
    events/
        events.go
    endings/
        endings.go
    content/
        loader.go
    ui/
        cli.go
```

This is the **target module tree**, adopted per D3. The current tree is `cmd/chronicle/ + internal/{action, conversation, core, integration, intent, lineage, llm, narrator, persistence, repl, simulation, tick, worldpack}`. The tree mismatch is recorded as a known v1→v2 migration gap and is **not** fixed in this commit (it would be code-migration work, which is explicitly out of scope).

**What this spec requires in the rewrite:** the pasted-doc tree is reproduced verbatim in the rewritten `ARCHITECTURE.md`. The README's "Project Layout" section is reconciled to show "_V2 target tree_ (current code is v1 sim; module migration tracked in PHASES.md Phase 34.x)".

### 5.2 The pasted doc's persistence §18 ("JSON serialization. One file. No migrations until v2.1.")

The current code uses SQLite (`modernc.org/sqlite`), per-branch SQLite files, branches directory, replay, diff. Per D7, the doc states plain JSON one-file. This **contradicts the current code**.

**What this spec requires in the rewrite:** the doc states plain JSON one-file. The README's CLI examples section removes all `.db` subcommands (`save`, `resume`, `info`, `diff`) and replaces them with v2's simpler save/load model (described in ARCHITECTURE.md §18, lifted verbatim from the pasted doc).

### 5.3 The pasted doc's persistence does not name a save-file extension

The pasted doc says "Save file … JSON serialization. One file." but does not specify a file extension. **Default per this spec: `.json` on disk.** The README's CLI examples can use `mysave.json`.

### 5.4 The pasted doc's `internal/llm` and `chronicle doctor`

Per D5, the v2 doc does not mention LLM. **Verification:** search the rewritten arch/README/PHASES/doc-substitutes for `OpenCode`, `OPENCODE_ZEN`, `llm/`, `narrator`, `intent`, `doctor`, `World AI`, `per-tick callback` — none of these strings should appear in v2 docs.

### 5.5 The pasted doc's `internal/simulation` references and engine dep

The current code has the LLM-boundary invariant ("`internal/simulation` MUST NOT import `internal/llm`"). Per D4, simulation engines are retired in the doc. **Verification:** search the rewritten docs for `Population Engine`, `Relationship Engine`, `Marriage Engine`, `Memory Engine`, `Goal Engine`, `Economy Engine`, `Event Engine`, `Tier 2`, `Tier 3` — only a single historical acknowledgement may remain (see §5.6).

### 5.6 Single permitted historical acknowledgement of the retired v1 sim layer

The new ARCHITECTURE.md MAY include a one-paragraph "Status" note after §22 (Engine Architecture) reading roughly:

> **Status (2026-06-17):** The current Chronicle codebase still implements the v1 simulation design (deterministic 7-engine tick loop, 150-NPC autonomous goal/relationship/memory/marriage/economy/event engines, LLM-first narration, SQLite per-branch persistence). This ARCHITECTURE.md describes the v2 design that the codebase will be migrated to. The migration is tracked in `PHASES.md`. The Q3 2026 target is v2 playable end-to-end.

This is the only mention. All other v1-specific prose (engine order, RNG streams, hash methodology, replay tests, etc.) is gone.

### 5.7 README "Project Layout" section

Reconciled to show **two** trees: the v2 target tree (canonical), and an "actual/current code" footnote that points to PHASES.md for the migration backlog.

### 5.8 README "Determinism" section

Replaced with a one-paragraph redirect to ARCHITECTURE.md's embedded determinism section (see §7 below).

### 5.9 README "What's in the box" — factions

Replaced with v2 factions: Kingdom, Mages, Dragons, Underworld (per D10, named numbers and factions are aspirational but the pasted doc's faction set is part of the canon).

### 5.10 README "20+ verbs" and "45+ natural language aliases"

Removed entirely. Per D6, v2 has no free-text commands.

### 5.11 Hyperlink integrity

The current README links to `PHASES.md` (one place) and the current ARCHITECTURE.md links to `docs/DETERMINISM.md` and `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` (in the preamble). Per this spec:

- `README.md → ./PHASES.md` link: preserved (PHASES.md is replaced, not deleted; the link target still resolves).
- `ARCHITECTURE.md → ./docs/DETERMINISM.md` link: removed (D11 — determinism is embedded).
- `ARCHITECTURE.md → ./SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` link: removed (§4.4 — tick spec is deleted).
- `PHASES.md → ./ARCHITECTURE.md` link: preserved (canonical spec target).

After the rewrite, no `grep -n '\](\./' *.md docs/*.md` line should reference a deleted file (`docs/DETERMINISM.md` redirect-stubs are OK; `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` is fully gone).

---

## 6. The Pivoted Doc Stack (After This Commit)

After the rewrite, the doc stack is:

```
chronicle/
├── ARCHITECTURE.md              # v2 spec (the user-pasted 25-section doc + §6 acknowledgment)
├── README.md                    # framing for v2 (banner, what's in the box, v2 module tree, build/test/run)
├── PHASES.md                    # v2 phase plan (Phase 34+ backlog; v1 sim-engine retirement; content authoring)
├── SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md      # DELETED
├── docs/
│   └── DETERMINISM.md           # 1-paragraph redirect stub (or deleted)
└── chronicle-v2-pivot-spec.md   # THIS FILE — preserved as historical record of the pivot decision
```

The new `PHASES.md` should at minimum include (as a starting skeleton — content detail is fleshed-out in follow-on phases, see suggest_followups):

- **Phase 34.** v1 → v2 docs sweep (this commit, including this spec file).
- **Phase 35.** v1 engine retirement. Delete `internal/simulation`, `internal/llm`, `internal/narrator`, `internal/intent`. Trim `internal/repl`, `internal/worldpack`. (Per D4.)
- **Phase 36.** v2 module scaffold. Create `internal/{engine, story, state, events, endings, content, ui}`, migrate package boundaries. (Per D3.)
- **Phase 37.** v2 content YAML schema. Author the actual file format for story nodes / choices / effects / conditions / events / endings / character profiles / flags / variables / inventory.
- **Phase 38.** v2 content authoring. Begin authoring Act 1 + Act 2 story content, ~70 nodes, destined for `content/acts/act1.yaml` and `content/acts/act2.yaml` (per pasted-doc §21).
- **Phase 39.** v2 save/load. Replace SQLite with the JSON serialization (per D7).
- **Phase 40.** v2 acceptance. Full playthrough from start to a v2 ending; 12 endings reachable; 4 protagonists reachable; 3 romance targets reachable.

(PHASES.md may end up assigning these differently — that's a follow-up phase. This spec just mandates that the new PHASES.md does NOT carry the v1-era phase list.)

---

## 7. Determinism Section Embedded in ARCHITECTURE.md (per D11)

The new `ARCHITECTURE.md` will gain a new section (call it **§18A: Determinism & Save Round-Trip**) placed before or after §18 (Save System). Sketch (the actual phrasing is left to the drafter, but the shape and content must match):

```text
# §18A. Determinism & Save Round-Trip

**Status:** Chronicle v2 determinism contract.

**Scope.** Unlike the v1 simulation, the v2 authored-content engine has
no runtime RNG in the player path. Choice resolution is deterministic
given the saved `WorldState` (a `SaveGame` JSON object) and the authored
story-graph content (`content/`). Narration text is the authored text on
the node — there is no procedural prose, no LLM prose, no template RNG.

**Determinism invariants.**

1. Save/load round-trip. `SaveGame → disk → SaveGame` is a literal
   byte-stable round trip. A load produces a `WorldState` that compares
   equal to the saved state under struct-equality after JSON
   normalize/canonicalize.

2. Story traversal determinism. Given a fixed `SaveGame` and fixed
   authored content, the engine produces the same `NodeID → ChoiceID →
   Effect[]` traversal every run. Authored content is content-addressed
   (hashed) at load; mismatches in content are a hard error, not silent
   divergence.

3. Versioning and migration are explicit. The save's `Version` field gates
   load-time migration. No silent migration. The migration code lives
   in `internal/content/migrate.go` (or equivalent v2 path).

**Forbidden patterns in v2 player-path code.**

- `math/rand` global functions.
- `time.Now()` as a state-mutating input.
- `crypto/rand` for game state (use only for non-game entropy like save
  ID generation).

**Forbidden patterns in v2 content.**

- LLM-generated prose embedded in node `Text` fields.
- Procedurally generated choices or effects.

**Hash (for reference).** The v2 save's `WorldHash(w)` is a SHA256 of the
canonicalized `SaveGame` JSON (sorted keys, normalized numbers). It is
used for save-load regression tests, NOT for cross-simulation
reproducibility (v2 has no simulation in the player path).
```

(Length ~30 lines. Compare to the existing `docs/DETERMINISM.md`'s 460 lines, almost all of which are v1 sim-specific.)

---

## 8. Commit & Push Spec

### 8.1 Single commit on `main`, no PR

Per D9. The commit is direct on `main`. There is no PR. This is consistent with the recent commit history (multiple "Phase X" commits have landed directly on `main`).

### 8.2 Suggested commit message

Following the recent commit-message convention ("Phase N: <verb> <object>"):

```
Phase 34: Pivot documentation to Chronicle v2 (choice-driven branching adventure)

- ARCHITECTURE.md: rewritten as the v2 CYOA-authored branching adventure spec
- README.md: rewritten framing around choice menu, 4 protagonists, 5 acts, 12 endings
- PHASES.md: rewritten for v2 phase plan (Phase 34+ migration backlog)
- SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md: deleted (no sim tick in v2; Runtime Flow in ARCHITECTURE §23)
- docs/DETERMINISM.md: stubbed / slimmed; full v2 determinism contract lives in ARCHITECTURE §18A

Code is unchanged in this commit; v1 simulation code remains in tree
under PHASES Phase 35 (engine retirement) and Phase 36 (v2 module scaffold).

See chronicle-v2-pivot-spec.md for the binding plan behind this commit.

Decisions captured:
  D1 Full pivot - docs first, code later
  D2 Dragon/Elara/Void Dragon kept as canonical named example content
  D3 Adopt new module tree (cmd/ + internal/{engine,story,state,events,endings,content,ui})
  D4 Sim engines explicitly retired in doc
  D5 Strict 'no AI' - LLM/narrator/intent/doctor purged from doc
  D6 Pure choice menu - no free text input
  D7 Plain JSON save file, no migrations until v2.1
  D8 All 5 stakeholder docs replaced
  D9 Single commit
  D10 Numeric specifics (4 protags, 5 acts, 12 endings) as aspirational
  D11 Determinism embedded in ARCHITECTURE §18A; docs/DETERMINISM slimmed
```

### 8.3 Push command

```
git push origin main
```

No force-push. No tags. No additional branches.

### 8.4 Pre-commit verification checklist (run before `git commit`)

In order:

1. **Render the rewrites in a Markdown previewer** (or `cat` them) — verify there are no broken internal doc-links (see §5.11). `grep -n '\](\./' ARCHITECTURE.md README.md PHASES.md docs/DETERMINISM.md` should return only valid targets.
2. **Search for retired strings** — `grep -n -E -i 'population engine|relationship engine|marriage engine|memory engine|goal engine|economy engine|event engine|math/rand|OPENCODE_ZEN|tick|worldhash|replay|core.worldhash|tier 2|tier 3'` in the rewritten docs. **Only** the §5.6 acknowledgement paragraph may match. The rest must be clean.
3. **Confirm `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` is removed from the working tree** — `ls -la SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` should fail.
4. **Confirm `docs/DETERMINISM.md`** is either the stub or deleted — if the stub is used, grep it for any v1-specific line (`Population → Relationship → Marriage → Memory → Goal → Economy → Event`, etc.) — must not appear.
5. **Confirm `chronicle-v2-pivot-spec.md`** is present and untracked initially — add it explicitly to the commit.
6. **Run `git status`** — verify exactly the right files (+ 1 new file, - 1 file, ~4 modifications).

### 8.5 Post-push verification

```
git log -1 --pretty=fuller    # verify commit landed
git ls-remote origin main     # verify push landed
```

---

## 9. Risks & Pitfalls (Top 12)

These are the issues most likely to bite during execution, ordered by severity.

1. **Internal doc-link rot.** README → PHASES link. PHASES → ARCHITECTURE link. ARCHITECTURE → DETERMINISM link must be removed. SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC link must be removed. A broken `[text](./docs/DETERMINISM.md)` in the rewritten ARCHITECTURE will silently 404.
2. **Stranded §5.6 acknowledgement.** If the rewrite forgets to include the one-paragraph v1→v2 status note, contributors landing on the file with no preamble may be confused about why v1 sim is still in `internal/`. The note is the only honest bridge.
3. **Mismatched module tree presentation.** README's "Project Layout" section must NOT show the v2 tree as if it were the current tree, or contributors will assume the migration is done. Use the dual-tree reconciliation from §5.7.
4. **Persisted `.db` references lingering.** If the README's CLI Examples block keeps `./chronicle save -out myrun.db` lines, that contradicts D7. Strip all `.db` references.
5. **LLM mentions finding a back door.** Search for the strings in §5.4 across the rewrites — `intent parser`, `narrator`, `LLM`, `OpenCode`, `doctor`. A single stray mention in a doc-comment-like context will undermine the strict "no AI" stance (D5).
6. **Free-text REPL commands finding a back door.** Search for `> look`, `> walk`, `> talk`, `> travel`, `> inspect`, `> search`, `> pray` in the rewritten docs. The PHASES §6 "Status after Phase 33" block at the tail of the current PHASES.md has a long REPL walkthrough that must be deleted wholesale (D6).
7. **PHASES.md phase-numbering conflicts.** Current PHASES.md has duplicate Phase 34 entries (`--no-llm`) and duplicate Phase 35 entries (`World AI` and `XDG world directory`). The v2 doc has no need for either. Cleaning this up in the rewrite is straightforward.
8. **Numeric specifics in the pasted doc treated as binding.** Per D10, the "4 protagonists / 5 acts / 12 endings / 3 romance targets" are aspirational; if the README promotes them to "v2 DoD" or "v2 acceptance test" without the aspirational framing, contributors may treat them as hard floors. Use the framing language from the pasted doc (§20, §25) verbatim.
9. **Module path mismatch with `go.mod`.** The current module path is `github.com/chronicle-dev/chronicle` (per ARCHITECTURE.md Part X). The pasted doc does not assert a module path. The new ARCHITECTURE.md should keep `github.com/chronicle-dev/chronicle` as-is (or, as an open item in PHASES, mark it for final-org pickup later).
10. **Test/doc-snippet coupling.** If any test file in `internal/integration/*_test.go` asserts on a string in `PHASES.md` or `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md`, deleting or rewriting those files will fail those tests. **Mitigation:** grep test files for `PHASES.md`, `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md` content — no test currently writes/asserts doc strings (this needs verification in the execution phase). If any test does, update it.
11. **`docs/DETERMINISM.md` redirect-stub drift.** If the stub file is left in place with v1 content, it becomes a quiet landmine. Either replace the body with the stub or delete the file. The spec prefers the stub, but either is acceptable per §4.5.
12. **Commit-message scope creep.** The commit message is intentionally long (it captures the 11 pinned decisions). Resist pulling additional doc sweeps (Makefile, CI config, etc.) into this commit. A clean, single-purpose commit is easier to revert/cherry-pick than a sprawling one.

---

## 10. Out of Scope (Explicit Non-Goals of This Commit)

- **Code refactor.** No `internal/...` package is touched.
- **Removing v1 simulation packages.** `internal/simulation`, `internal/llm`, `internal/narrator`, `internal/intent`, `internal/worldpack`, `internal/repl` remain in tree.
- **Authoring story content.** No files written under `content/`.
- **CHANGELOG.md / RELEASE.md / license decision.** "License: TBD." carries over unchanged.
- **CI / build / Makefile changes.** Build commands (`make build`, `make test`) stay as documented in README §Build, Test, Run.
- **Tagging or releasing.** This is a `main`-branch commit only.
- **Adding a CHRONICLE_LICENSE or selecting an open-source license.** The current docs say "License: TBD." and the v2 doc carries that forward unchanged.

---

## 11. Acceptance — Done Means

This spec's execution is "done" when:

1. `ARCHITECTURE.md` matches the user-pasted 25-section text (with §5 reconciliation edits, the §5.6 acknowledgement note, and the §7 determinism section).
2. `README.md` is a v2 framing of the project: choice menu, 4 protagonists, 5 acts, 12 endings, Dragon/Elara/Selene/Orion/Void Dragon named subjects, no LLM references, no REPL free-text verbs.
3. `PHASES.md` is a v2 phase plan starting at Phase 34; the v1 phase list is gone; the §6 suggested skeleton is applied.
4. `SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` is removed.
5. `docs/DETERMINISM.md` is either the §4.5 stub or deleted.
6. `chronicle-v2-pivot-spec.md` is committed alongside the rewrites.
7. The pre-commit checklist in §8.4 passes six for six.
8. The commit message follows §8.2.
9. `git push origin main` succeeds.
10. `git log -1` on origin/main contains the commit.

When those ten are checked, the pivot is closed. The next phase (whatever it is — likely getting user sign-off and starting Phase 35 engine retirement from PHASES.md) is a follow-up.

---

## 12. Open Items (Recorded in PHASES.md Phase 34 Follow-ups)

These are tracked for future phases, **NOT** resolved in this commit:

- Pick final org/repo name (currently `github.com/chronicle-dev/chronicle`).
- Pick license (currently TBD).
- Reconcile module path with go.mod if the org changes.
- Author the actual `content/acts/act1.yaml` through `act5.yaml`.
- Author `content/protagonists/*.yaml` (Kael, Lyra, Raven, Aria).
- Author `content/companions/*.yaml` (Elara, Selene, Orion, etc.).
- Author `content/endings.yaml` (12 endings).
- Decide whether dependency `modernc.org/sqlite` is dropped from go.sum as part of Phase 39.
- Decide whether the `internal/repl` package is deprecated in Phase 35 or kept as a terminal-only shell for the v2 command-line tool.

(These are tracked explicitly so they can be picked up by a future task without re-discovery.)

---

## 13. Quick-Reference Decision Matrix

A consolidated lookup of every decision in one place. **If something in this spec contradicts the table below, the table wins.**

| Area | Choice |
|---|---|
| Code changes in this commit | None |
| Files rewritten | ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md, PHASES.md |
| Files deleted | SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md |
| Files reduced to stub | docs/DETERMINISM.md (acceptable; deletion also acceptable) |
| Files added | chronicle-v2-pivot-spec.md |
| Commit count | 1 |
| Branch | main |
| PR | None |
| Force-push | No |
| Tags / releases | No |
| Authoritative input | User-pasted 25-section document |
| Module tree provenance | Adopted as v2 target; v1 code tree noted in README / PHASES |
| Engine deprecation narrative | One acknowledgement paragraph only (per §5.6) |
| LLM mentions in v2 docs | None (search-confirmed) |
| Free-text REPL mentions in v2 docs | None (search-confirmed) |
| Save format | JSON, one file, no migrations until v2.1 |
| Persisted `.db` mentions in v2 docs | None |
| Determinism doc location | ARCHITECTURE.md §18A (embedded); docs/DETERMINISM.md stub or deleted |
| Determinism doc length | ~30 lines |
| Content count (protag / acts / endings) | Aspirational (treated as working figures) |
| Romance targets | Elara, Selene, Orion (canonical, content-team favorite at this revision) |
| Factions | Kingdom, Mages, Dragons, Underworld (aspirational framing) |
| Status note | v1→v2 one-paragraph acknowledgement in ARCHITECTURE.md after §22 |

---

## 14. Execution Phase — Summary Cheat Sheet

When the user approves this spec, the execution phase is:

1. Open `command/chronicle-claude-cc` or `text editor`.
2. `write_file` ARCHITECTURE.md → user-pasted 25 sections + §5.6 ack + §7 determinism.
3. `write_file` README.md → v2 framing per §4.2.
4. `write_file` PHASES.md → v2 phase plan per §4.3 + §6.2.
5. `write_file` docs/DETERMINISM.md → §4.5 stub (or skip and prepare delete).
6. `git rm SIMULATION_TICK_SPEC.md` (or `rm` then `git add -A`).
7. `write_file` chronicle-v2-pivot-spec.md → THIS FILE copied to repo root.
8. Verify the §8.4 checklist (six for six).
9. `git add -A`.
10. `git commit -m "...message per §8.2..."`.
11. `git push origin main`.

End of spec. Stop. Execution is gated on user approval.
